content: The iterative self-feedback loop of the original Self-Refine framework can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent reflection perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms to eliminate blind spots in single-perspective self-review, creating a multi-iteration multi-perspective refinement system that maintains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data while fixing its common flaw of getting stuck in local self-reinforcement of incorrect initial outputs. This combined framework can improve code generation and general output quality without adding computational or annotation overhead that plagues most advanced reflection methods.
